topProblems.title: Matching riders to nearby available drivers must feel instant or the hail habit wins
Sparse driver density in a new city creates long ETAs and cancelled trips

features.title: Geo-fenced matching with live driver locations and ETA
Driver app to accept trips, navigate, and confirm drop-off
Rider app to request, track, and rate the trip

earlyAdopters.title: SF, Soho, and Chelsea test zones

statements.title: Request to car-en-route in under a few minutes in covered zones
statements.detail: Operational promise the dispatch stack must keep — not a marketing tagline

highLevelConcepts.title: Air-traffic control for street rides
highLevelConcepts.detail: X-for-Y for ops/engineering: match supply to demand in real time across a geo-fenced city

returns.title: Completed trips attributable to dispatch quality

advantages.title: City-by-city density know-how
advantages.detail: Which neighborhoods to seed first, when to subsidize drivers, and how to read cancel spikes

advantages.title: Dispatch heuristics competitors re-learn per market
advantages.detail: ETA models, match radius, and surge triggers tuned from live trip data — not copy-pasteable from another city
